input.background: In the spring of 1989, physicians and pharmacists at the University of Wisconsin Hospital clinics became aware of an increase in the frequency an adverse reaction (cerebellar toxicity) to a drug used in bone marrow transplant patients. This association became apparent after the hospital switched from the original formulation of the drug to a generic equivalent. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration was contacted, and a team of investigators was sent to look into the problem. A subsequent record review confirmed the increased frequency of this serious adverse reaction.
input.question: Of the 25 patients treated with the generic form of the drug, 11(44%) experienced cerebellar toxicity. In comparison, 3(8.8%) of 34 individuals treated with the brandname product experienced toxicity. Thus, the relative risk associated with the generic drug was 44.0%/8.8%=5.0(P=0.002).

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

number_of_patients_generic.description: Number of patients treated with the generic form of the drug.

number_of_patients_brandname.description: Number of patients treated with the brandname form of the drug.

toxicity_generic.description: Number of patients experiencing cerebellar toxicity after using the generic form of the drug.

toxicity_brandname.description: Number of patients experiencing cerebellar toxicity after using the brandname form of the drug.

toxicity_rate_generic.description: Percentage of patients experiencing cerebellar toxicity after using the generic form of the drug.

toxicity_rate_brandname.description: Percentage of patients experiencing cerebellar toxicity after using the brandname form of the drug.

relative_risk.description: The relative risk of cerebellar toxicity associated with the generic drug compared to the brandname drug.

p_value.description: The p-value indicating the statistical significance of the difference in toxicity rates between the generic and brandname drugs.
